outcome_contract.why: Two booking pages display wrong WhatsApp CTA labels. Bug B (DoubleRoomBookContent) is visually broken today; Bug A (ApartmentBookContent) is fragile and will break silently if section-specific translations diverge.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Both WhatsApp CTA buttons on the apartment booking and double-room booking pages use semantically correct translation keys, eliminating label drift risk.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Bug B renders "More About This Room" on a WhatsApp button — visually wrong
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Users see wrong label on double-room booking page
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Confirm rendered label changes correctly post-fix

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No state change; WhatsApp link behavior unchanged
No auth, input, or data handling change
One test (`ga4-07-apartment-checkout.test.tsx`) uses `data-testid="whatsapp-cta"` — unaffected by key change
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No new tests needed; existing test still valid
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Confirm no test assertions on text content

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `bookPage.json` adds one new root-level key across 18 locales
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Must add to all 18 locales; missing key falls back to EN (acceptable)

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Pure i18n key lookup; no hot path change
Standard Next.js deploy; no feature flags needed

stage_payload.scope_summary: Two private-rooms booking pages use wrong i18n translation keys for their WhatsApp CTA buttons. - **Bug A (ApartmentBookContent):** Uses `t("streetLevelArrival.whatsappCta")` within the `apartmentPage` namespace. This references a `streetLevelArrival` section key on an apartment booking page. Currently returns the same text ("WhatsApp for quick answers") but is semantically wrong and fragile: if `streetLevelArrival.whatsappCta` is ever made specific to street-level-arrival guidance, the apartment booking page would silently inherit the wrong label. The correct key is `t("book.whatsappCta")`, w
goals: Fix `ApartmentBookContent.tsx:272` to use `t("book.whatsappCta")` (correct semantic key, same text, no locale changes needed)
Fix `DoubleRoomBookContent.tsx:259` to use `tBook("whatsappCta")` after adding the key to `bookPage.json` in all 18 locales
non_goals: Adding new translation infrastructure or locale coverage tooling
Addressing the vi/zh machine translation quality issues (pre-existing)
constraints: Must not change rendered text in any locale (same values, just semantically correct keys)
Must not break existing tests — tests use `data-testid="whatsapp-cta"` selector, not text content
Must add `whatsappCta` to all 18 locale `bookPage.json` files (not just EN)
assumptions: `apartmentPage.book.whatsappCta` is the canonical source of the translated WhatsApp CTA text
Adding `whatsappCta` at the root of `bookPage.json` is the right scope (generic booking fallback, not room-type-specific)

analysis_readiness.Test landscape confirmed: no assertions on text content, no test changes needed

process_topology.note: None: local code path only. This change affects only i18n key resolution in two client components. No multi-step process, CI/deploy lane, approval path, or operator runbook is affected.
